name: unreal-engine
description: Connect Artyx to Unreal Engine 5.8's native in-editor MCP server for editor automation, actors, Blueprints, level composition, and verification loops.

interface.shortDescription: Automate the Unreal Editor: actors, Blueprints, levels.
interface.longDescription: Operate a live Unreal Editor over MCP through Unreal Engine 5.8's native in-editor MCP server: spawn actors, build and compile Blueprints, compose levels, configure lighting, inspect Slate widgets, and run automation tests. Connects to the editor's local HTTP endpoint — no third-party server to install. The bundled skill leans into list-first, compile-then-spawn discipline because editor writes silently no-op more than any other DCC.
defaultPrompt: Build a small Unreal level with a floor, three lit hero actors, and a compiled BP_Spinner that rotates on Tick.

companion.title: Enable Unreal Engine's native MCP server
companion.summary: Turn on the built-in 'Unreal MCP' plugin (UE 5.8+) and start its local server. Defaults are 127.0.0.1 and port 8000 — Artyx will ask for these on install.
steps: In Unreal Engine 5.8+: Edit → Plugins, search 'Unreal MCP' (plugin id ModelContextProtocol), tick Enabled, and restart the editor when prompted.

Start the server: Edit → Editor Preferences → General → Model Context Protocol → enable 'Auto Start Server' (off by default), or run 'ModelContextProtocol.StartServer' in the editor console on demand.
Keep a project open. The server defaults to 127.0.0.1:8000 (HTTP + SSE, loopback). Enter the host and port when Artyx prompts during install.
